source_jobs.title: Information Security & IT Senior Manager
source_jobs.description:
About Us

Our software solves the most critical emerging grid integration challenges to ensure that the impending energy transition is clean, equitable, and resilient. Our enterprise solutions help the grid absorb the electrification wave with ease. Utilizing modern, cloud-native platform architecture and robust systems optimization, WeaveGrid’s software is built from the ground up to tackle the most critical network challenges while meeting the stringent regulatory, security, and reliability requirements of the utility industry.

About the Job

WeaveGrid is looking for a mission-driven, highly independent, action-oriented information security and information technology professional to oversee its InfoSec and IT functions. You will oversee the company’s IT vendor(s), security and compliance, and drive AI and technology adoption within the business.

Key responsibilities include:

 - Own and execute WeaveGrid's information security and IT program end-to-end — this is a high-impact, hands-on IC role.

 - Maintain cloud security posture across AWS (IAM governance, configuration review, cloud-native security tooling)

 - Own the corporate security control environment — EDR, email security, identity governance, network monitoring, DLP

 - Oversee strategy and day-to-day management of our IT services contractor(s). Ensure operational excellence and intervene as needed to ensure timely responses to internal stakeholders.

 - Serve as the internal technical owner of the corporate IT environment and escalation point for security-intersecting IT issues

 - Manage SOC 2 Type II compliance across all five Trust Service Criteria, including auditor relationships and evidence collection

 - Support CCPA and privacy compliance by monitoring and managing data subject access requests and the associated overarching process, applicable website technologies, and ad hoc privacy requests, in partnership with the Legal team.

 - Own application security for our web and mobile products, including coordinating annual penetration tests and driving remediation with Engineering.

 - Run the vulnerability management program — prioritization, tracking, and reporting.

 - Manage incident response — maintain the IR plan, run tabletop exercises, and handle real incidents through to post-mortem.

 - Maintain and test BC/DR plans in coordination with Engineering and Operations.

 - Conduct and document quarterly access reviews across critical systems.

 - Review security terms in vendor and customer contracts; complete customer security questionnaires; run vendor risk assessments.

 - Help administer the personnel security program (security awareness training, onboarding/offboarding controls) in partnership with the People team.

 - Lead the company’s Information Security & IT Committee — evaluating tools, defining acceptable use, and managing data exposure risk across sanctioned tools, including AI platforms

 - Drive AI and new technology adoption within WeaveGrid, engaging internal and external stakeholders as applicable

 
 

About You

Qualifications

Required

 - 7+ years in information security with meaningful program ownership experience

 - Experience at a growth-stage startup company, ideally as a primary security practitioner

 - Experience providing or overseeing IT services

 - Obsessed with client service and meeting internal SLAs

 - Hands-on SOC 2 Type II experience — you've run an audit, not just supported one.

 - Familiarity with leading security and compliance governance tools (i.e. Vanta, Drata, OneTrust).

 - Working knowledge of CCPA and US data privacy requirements

 - Experience reviewing contractual security requirements.

 - Deep interest in and knowledge of leading AI tooling, as applicable to early-stage startups.

 - Proven track record driving security, compliance, and/or technology change within a fast-paced organization.

 - Familiarity with AI security risks and SaaS AI governance

Preferred

 - Experience or familiarity with IS

source_jobs.title: Director, Regulatory Affairs & Market Development
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT THE ROLE

In this role, you will help accelerate the adoption of utility EV managed charging and virtual power plant programs by shaping the regulatory, policy, and market conditions that enable WeaveGrid’s growth.

The ideal candidate is a hybrid policy strategist and market builder: someone who can shape markets externally, build coalitions, manage consultants, brief internal teams, and turn regulatory developments into commercial insight. You will work closely with Business Development, ClientSuccess, Product, Marketing, Legal, and executive leadership to ensure WeaveGrid is positioned to win in priority markets and help utilities unlock the full grid value of EVs and other distributed energy resources.




RESPONSIBILITIES

Primary responsibilities include:


REGULATORY STRATEGY & MARKET DEVELOPMENT

 - Develop and execute WeaveGrid’s regulatory and policy engagement strategy across priority North American markets.

 - Identify, monitor, and prioritize high-leverage regulatory proceedings, legislative developments, utility planning processes, and market design initiatives relevant to transportation electrification, managed charging, VPPs, DER flexibility, rate design, distribution planning, DSM, and grid modernization.

 - Translate regulatory and policy developments into actionable recommendations for WeaveGrid’s commercial strategy, client engagement, product roadmap, and market prioritization.

 - Support market entry and expansion strategies by identifying policy tailwinds, regulatory barriers, utility program opportunities, and potential coalition partners.

 - Support utility program approvals and expansions by helping clients and internal teams navigate regulatory processes.

 - Identify new business models, program designs, and partnership opportunities emerging from federal, state, and utility-level policy initiatives.


REGULATORY ENGAGEMENT, ADVOCACY & THOUGHT LEADERSHIP

 - Draft, review, and coordinate regulatory comments, testimony, white papers, policy briefs, and other advocacy materials.

 - Represent WeaveGrid in regulatory proceedings, stakeholder workshops, industry convenings, and trade associations while building relationships with regulators, policymakers, utilities, advocates, NGOs, and other stakeholders.

 - Partner with Marketing to create thought leadership materials, conference content, case studies, and other public-facing narratives that strengthen WeaveGrid’s market position.


COALITION BUILDING & CONSULTANT MANAGEMENT

 - Identify opportunities for WeaveGrid to work through coalitions, trade associations, industry groups, and aligned partners to advance shared policy objectives.

 - Cultivate and manage outside consultants, attorneys, lobbyists, and policy advisors to support market monitoring, regulatory filings, stakeholder engagement, and advocacy strategy.


INTERNAL INTELLIGENCE & CROSS-FUNCTIONAL ENABLEMENT

 - Serve as WeaveGrid’s internal expert on regulatory and policy developments affecting priority markets, customers, and commercial opportunities.

 - Brief Business Development, Client Success, Product, Marketing, Legal, and executive stakeholders on relevant policy developments, market signals, and implications for WeaveGrid.

 - Create repeatable internal processes for tracking proceedings, summarizing developments, flagging risks and opportunities, and enabling commercial teams to act on regulatory intelligence.

 - Partner with Business Development teams to support RFPs, proposals, bid defenses, customer conversations, account plan updates, and new market development.

 - Partner with Client Success teams to support existing utility clients navigating regulatory approvals, filings, program design changes, evaluation requirements, or stakeholder engagement.

 - Partner with Product and Solutions teams to ensure regulatory trends, utility planning needs, and market requirements inform product strategy and roadmap decisions.


ABOUT YOU

 - You are a strategic policy and

source_jobs.title: Senior Manager / Director, Marketing and Communications
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Our software solves the most critical emerging grid integration challenges to ensure that the impending energy transition is clean, equitable, and resilient. Our enterprise solutions help the grid absorb the coming electrification wave with ease. Utilizing modern, cloud-native platform architecture and robust systems optimization, WeaveGrid’s software is built from the ground up to tackle the most critical network challenges while meeting the stringent regulatory, security, and reliability requirements of the utility industry.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We are looking for a strategic, hands-on marketing leader to help define and scale the next phase of WeaveGrid’s B2B marketing function. This person will lead a broad marketing portfolio spanning positioning, product marketing, brand, communications, content, events, sales enablement, and targeted demand generation. This role also plays a key part in supporting fundraising and investor-facing communications, ensuring WeaveGrid's narrative is well-positioned with the investment community.

This is a high-impact role for someone who can both shape strategy and execute with excellence. You will work closely with leaders across the executive team, Sales, Partnerships, Policy, Product, and Client Success to clarify how WeaveGrid shows up in the market and ensure our marketing directly supports enterprise growth.

The ideal candidate is a strong B2B marketer and exceptional communicator who can translate complex technical, regulatory, and commercial topics into clear market narratives, compelling sales materials, and campaigns that reach the right utility, automotive, regulatory, and partner audiences.




CORE RESPONSIBILITIES

Tasks include, but are not limited to:

 - Strategic Marketing & Positioning: Lead WeaveGrid’s market narrative, messaging, and strategic marketing priorities
   
   - Refine WeaveGrid’s company narrative, category story, and differentiated positioning
   
   - Develop clear messaging for utility, automotive, regulatory, partner, investor, and executive audiences
   
   - Translate complex topics, including managed charging, grid flexibility, OEM integrations, customer enrollment, distribution value, and utility program design, into compelling market-facing narratives

 - Product Marketing & Sales Enablement: Build the materials and messaging that help WeaveGrid’s commercial teams win
   
   - Create and maintain core sales and BD materials in partnership with RevOps, including pitch decks, solution briefs, case studies, one-pagers, RFP language, competitive narratives, and executive leave-behinds
   
   - Partner with Product, Sales, Partnerships, Policy, and Client Success to package WeaveGrid’s capabilities, customer outcomes, program launches, and proof points
   
   - Support go-to-market strategy for new products, partnerships, integrations, utility programs, and market expansions
   
   - Develop messaging, collateral, and competitive intelligence, including positioning benchmarks and battlecards, that help move strategic accounts through long, complex enterprise buying cycles

 - Brand, Communications & Thought Leadership: Ensure WeaveGrid shows up consistently and credibly in the market
   
   - Build and manage a focused content strategy tied to priority accounts, market moments, policy developments, industry events, and commercial goals
   
   - Turn internal expertise into external thought leadership, including articles, reports, webinars, conference narratives, LinkedIn content, speaking abstracts, and executive bylines
   
   - Partner with external agency support on media strategy, announcements, award submissions, and communications planning
   
   - Support company and partner announcements, customer launches, conference moments, and other market-facing communications

 - Events, Campaigns & Targeted Demand Generation: Build programs that reach and influence high-value audiences
   
   - Shape WeaveGrid’s event and conference strategy, inc

source_jobs.title: Senior Executive Assistant and Workplace Experience Manager
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Our software solves the most critical emerging grid integration challenges to ensure that the impending energy transition is clean, equitable, and resilient. Our enterprise solutions help the grid absorb the electrification wave with ease. Utilizing modern, cloud-native platform architecture and robust systems optimization, WeaveGrid’s software is built from the ground up to tackle the most critical network challenges while meeting the stringent regulatory, security, and reliability requirements of the utility industry.




ABOUT THE JOB

The Senior Executive Assistant and Workplace Experience Manager will play a critical role supporting WeaveGrid’s leadership and offices in San Francisco. The role will initially support the VP Policy and Marketing (the “VP”) and be responsible for fully managing the company’s headquarters in San Francisco. The role is highly cross-functional, high impact, and interactive with external stakeholders and the WeaveGrid team. This role is expected to work from WeaveGrid's San Francisco office 4 days per week. For Executive Assistant responsibilities, this role reports to the VP; for Office Management responsibilities, this role reports to the General Counsel and VP of Administration.



Executive Assistant Responsibilities

 - Provide administrative and operational support to the VP, including calendar management, travel coordination, meeting preparation, expense support, and follow-up tracking.

 - Prioritize meeting requests and ensure preparatory materials are completed on time and sufficiently.

 - Implement a framework based on best practices for the executive time, including preserving focus time and strategic time, and ensuring time is available for critical tasks agreed with the VP.

 - Coordinate events and travel on behalf of the VP, including client and stakeholder meetings, and conferences.

 - Handle last-minute changes and troubleshoot travel issues, real-time.

 - Prepare “travel memos” to ensure seamless logistics, and preparation for the VP.

 - At the VP’s discretion, attend certain meetings to support agendas, logistics, notes, and action-item tracking.

 - Build relationships with the leadership team and across the organization.

 - Lead special projects, at the VP’s discretion.

 - Maintain utmost care and confidentiality with respect to sensitive information.

 - Prioritize, multi-task, and handle a continuously evolving environment.

 - Ability and willingness to support at least one executive.



Office Management Responsibilities

 - Oversee day-to-day office operations to ensure smooth operations for San Francisco employees.

 - Directly manage an office porter providing office support, so long as the office porter continues to provide services to WeaveGrid.

 - Execute a daily office checklist for opening and closing.

 - Manage office vendor relationships.

 - Oversee general office maintenance and coordinate with contractors and building management as needed, including AV and security, with support of WeaveGrid’s IT team.

 - Manage the kitchen and office supply inventory.

 - Check mail and packages.

 - Manage the office telephone system and access, including answering the company telephone and facilitating guest arrivals and departures.

 - Plan and execute office events, onsites, meals.

 - Coordinate IT setup for company events.

 - Ensure compliance with safety protocols.

 - Manage office budget.

 - Own the workplace experience strategy as the team scales, anticipating space, equipment, and process needs ahead of headcount growth.

 - Partner with the People/Talent team on space planning and seating as the office grows.

 - Special projects and other duties as designated by your manager or assigned designee.
   


ABOUT YOU

 - 5+ years of experience as an Executive Assistant supporting senior leaders (VP-level or above), ideally including experience at a fast-growing startup

 - 2+ years experience owning office management, facilities, or workplace e

source_jobs.title: Senior Software Engineer Backend
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Our software solves the most critical emerging grid integration challenges to ensure that the impending energy transition is clean, equitable, and resilient. Our enterprise solutions help the grid absorb the coming electrification wave with ease. Utilizing modern, cloud-native platform architecture and robust systems optimization, WeaveGrid’s software is built from the ground up to tackle the most critical network challenges while meeting the stringent regulatory, security, and reliability requirements of the utility industry.


ABOUT THE JOB

WeaveGrid's clean energy SaaS products monitor and manage a growing portfolio of distributed energy resources, including electric vehicles and battery energy storage systems, on behalf of electric utilities. We are seeking a seasoned backend engineer to independently design, implement, and deploy software with our partner device manufacturers and utilities. In this role, you will contribute to the technical direction of the team and help raise the bar for engineering quality. The impact of your code will be broad, from deeply integrating with diverse device manufacturer APIs, to monitoring and understanding battery status, to scheduling and verifying optimal charge or discharge plans, to ingesting, aggregating, and integrating signals from various utility sources.

In this role, we will ask you to:

 - Design and produce high quality code for robustness, testability, and maintainability

 - Work with our Python backend, relational database schemas, and microservice architecture & develop deep ownership of your area of work, including its long-term health, reliability, and technical debt

 - Partner closely with Product Managers and business stakeholders to design and deliver new, scalable product features that expand our platform’s DERMS capabilities.

 - Collaborate with engineers to improve system reliability, data quality, effectiveness, and scalability across our product and codebase.

 - Own rollouts, instrumentation, alerting, and reliability monitoring in our production cloud environment

 - Create brand new capabilities with new partners while reusing and generalizing existing modules

 - Use Docker containers, Kubernetes, AWS, GitHub, Jira/Confluence, and CircleCI

 - Participate in on-call rotations, periodically (~once per quarter) serving as the frontline presence for monitoring, diagnosing, and triaging production service issues

 - Contribute to the growth of the team through mentorship, design reviews, and code reviews, and serve as an effective interviewer for engineering candidates

 - Coordinate with multiple teams across several codebases in the engineering organization

 - Contribute to team prioritization by identifying and proposing improvements to your area of work, including new capabilities, reliability investments, and technical debt reduction

 - Special projects and other duties as designated by your manager or assigned designee


ABOUT YOU

 - You are driven to use your skills and talents to help fix the climate crisis

 - You have a Bachelor’s Degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or a related field, or 5+ years of relevant engineering experience, with a demonstrated track record of delivering complex backend systems independently

 - If working remotely, you are available to collaborate with team members during standard US West Coast working hours

 - If you’re based in the San Francisco Bay Area, you will be working from our San Francisco office 2 days/week

 - You care about the craft of software and feel a sense of ownership about code

 - You leverage AI tools effectively in your workflow, including for coding, while maintaining the engineering judgment to critically evaluate and catch AI-generated errors or subtle incorrectness

 - You enjoy solving hard technical problems and are excited to tackle the complexity of software that interacts with the real world

 - You think in systems, independently scoping and delivering end-to-en
